White-tailed Deer:
Licence Type:
General - Bag Limit = One White-tailed Deer

Seasons:
Archery
Youth Muzzleloader
Muzzleloader
General (rifle)

General deer hunters who hunt white-tailed deer during the fall moose season must also possess a valid draw moose licence for the area they are hunting.

Canadian Resident and Non-Canadian Resident licences may be available for Archery, Muzzleloader and General (rifle) seasons.

All hunters must submit biological samples from white-tailed deer, mule deer, and elk harvested within the CWD Surveillance Zone to a Drop-off Depot within seven (7) days from the time of kill. Hunters have the option of submitting the entire head and upper neck of harvested animals, or, hunters may extract and submit the testable tissues themselves. Testable tissues for white-tailed deer include the medial retropharyngeal lymph nodes and lower jaw.

Antlers should be removed prior to sample drop-off; hunters will not receive their head or antlers back. When removing antlers a shallow v-notch cut should be made through the skull. This cut should be no deeper than the midpoint of the eye socket.

Seasons:

Canadian Resident and Non-Canadian Resident licences may be available for Archery, Muzzleloader and General (rifle) seasons.

Bowhunters who hunt white-tailed deer in GHA 18 during the archery elk season must also possess a valid elk archery tag for the area in which they are hunting

All hunters must submit biological samples from white-tailed deer, mule deer, and elk harvested within the CWD Surveillance Zone to a Drop-off Depot within seven (7) days from the time of kill. Hunters have the option of submitting the entire head and upper neck of harvested animals, or, hunters may extract and submit the testable tissues themselves. Testable tissues for white-tailed deer include the medial retropharyngeal lymph nodes and lower jaw.

Antlers should be removed prior to sample drop-off; hunters will not receive their head or antlers back. When removing antlers a shallow v-notch cut should be made through the skull. This cut should be no deeper than the midpoint of the eye socket.

Where the other Big Game hunting seasons are closed, a resident may hunt wolves or coyotes provided he/she is in possession of a used or unused white-tailed deer, elk, moose, black bear or caribou licence. Hunters who harvest a wolf in GHAs 18 - 18C, 23, 23A and 26 are requested to submit: the lower jaw, with teeth attached; a 2.5 sq. cm. (1”x1”) sample of hide with at least 50 guard hairs from the groin or upper hind leg; and a 5 cm. (2”) cube of muscle to any Manitoba Conservation Officer Service District Office. When submitting your sample, you will be expected to provide the date of kill, exact location of the kill, sex of the animal and contact information.

Mule Deer:
Licence Type:
General Mule Deer - Bag Limit = One Mule Deer
Second Mule Deer - Bag Limit = One Antlerless Mule Deer
Third Mule Deer - Bag Limit = One Antlerless Mule Deer

Seasons:
Archery
Muzzleloader
General (rifle)

All hunters must submit biological samples from white-tailed deer, mule deer, and elk harvested within the CWD Surveillance Zone to a Drop-off Depot within seven (7) days from the time of kill. Hunters have the option of submitting the entire head and upper neck of harvested animals, or, hunters may extract and submit the testable tissues themselves. Testable tissues for mule deer include the medial retropharyngeal lymph nodes and lower jaw.

Antlers should be removed prior to sample drop-off; hunters will not receive their head or antlers back. When removing antlers a shallow v-notch cut should be made through the skull. This cut should be no deeper than the midpoint of the eye socket.

White-tailed Deer:
Licence Type:
General - Bag Limit = One White-tailed Deer
Second Deer - Bag Limit = One Antlerless White-tailed Deer
Third Deer - Bag Limit = One Antlerless White-tailed Deer

Seasons:
Archery
Youth Muzzleloader
Muzzleloader
General (rifle)

Canadian Resident licences are available for Archery, Muzzleloader and General (rifle) seasons. Non-Canadian Resident licences may be available for the Archery season only.

All hunters must submit biological samples from white-tailed deer, mule deer, and elk harvested within the CWD Surveillance Zone to a Drop-off Depot within seven (7) days from the time of kill. Hunters have the option of submitting the entire head and upper neck of harvested animals, or, hunters may extract and submit the testable tissues themselves. Testable tissues for white-tailed deer include the medial retropharyngeal lymph nodes and lower jaw.

Antlers should be removed prior to sample drop-off; hunters will not receive their head or antlers back. When removing antlers a shallow v-notch cut should be made through the skull. This cut should be no deeper than the midpoint of the eye socket.

Submission of Biological Samples are Requested - Hunters who harvest a white-tailed deer in GHAs 17A, 26 or 36 are requested to submit the head to a Drop-Off Depot at the Lac du Bonnet, Pine Falls or Seven Sisters Manitoba Conservation Officer Service District Office. Hunters are asked not to remove the antlers before submitting the head. If the specimen is a male, hunters will be asked when submitting the head whether they wish to retain the antlers and arrangements will be made for their return. These samples can be frozen. These samples will be examined for the presence of brainworm.

Seasons:
Archery
Youth Muzzleloader
Muzzleloader
General (rifle)

Canadian Resident licences are available for Archery, Muzzleloader and General (rifle) seasons. Non-Canadian Resident licences may be available for the Archery season only.

All hunters must submit biological samples from white-tailed deer, mule deer, and elk harvested within the CWD Surveillance Zone to a Drop-off Depot within seven (7) days from the time of kill. Hunters have the option of submitting the entire head and upper neck of harvested animals, or, hunters may extract and submit the testable tissues themselves. Testable tissues for white-tailed deer include the medial retropharyngeal lymph nodes and lower jaw.

Antlers should be removed prior to sample drop-off; hunters will not receive their head or antlers back. When removing antlers a shallow v-notch cut should be made through the skull. This cut should be no deeper than the midpoint of the eye socket.

All hunters must submit biological samples from white-tailed deer, mule deer, and elk harvested within the CWD Surveillance Zone to a Drop-off Depot within seven (7) days from the time of kill. Hunters have the option of submitting the entire head and upper neck of harvested animals, or, hunters may extract and submit the testable tissues themselves. Testable tissues for white-tailed deer include the medial retropharyngeal lymph nodes and lower jaw.

Antlers should be removed prior to sample drop-off; hunters will not receive their head or antlers back. When removing antlers a shallow v-notch cut should be made through the skull. This cut should be no deeper than the midpoint of the eye socket.

Second Deer - Bag Limit = One Antlerless White-tailed Deer

Seasons:
Archery
Youth Muzzleloader
Muzzleloader
General (rifle)

Canadian Resident licences are available for Archery, Muzzleloader and General (rifle) seasons. Non-Canadian Resident licences may be available for the Archery season only.

All hunters must submit biological samples from white-tailed deer, mule deer, and elk harvested within the CWD Surveillance Zone to a Drop-off Depot within seven (7) days from the time of kill. Hunters have the option of submitting the entire head and upper neck of harvested animals, or, hunters may extract and submit the testable tissues themselves. Testable tissues for white-tailed deer include the medial retropharyngeal lymph nodes and lower jaw.

Antlers should be removed prior to sample drop-off; hunters will not receive their head or antlers back. When removing antlers a shallow v-notch cut should be made through the skull. This cut should be no deeper than the midpoint of the eye socket.
Black Bear: None
Gray Wolf and Coyote:
Bag Limit = One Wolf and One Coyote
Fall and winter seasons

Gray wolves and coyotes can be hunted under the authority of any Big Game licence. If hunting in a GHA while the area is open to other Big Game species, the wolf or coyote hunter must have a licence and unused game tag (personal or party), which is valid for that area, species and time period. Where the other Big Game hunting seasons are closed, a resident may hunt wolves or coyotes provided he/she is in possession of a used or unused white-tailed deer, elk, moose, black bear or caribou licence. The hunter’s big game licence number is all that is required to possess a wolf or coyote taken under the authority of that licence.

Canadian Resident and Non-Canadian Resident wolf and coyote hunting opportunity may be available. See the annual Hunting Guide for more information.

Second Mule Deer - Bag Limit = One Antlerless Mule Deer
Third Mule Deer - Bag Limit = One Antlerless Mule Deer

Seasons:
Archery
Muzzleloader
General (rifle)

All hunters must submit biological samples from white-tailed deer, mule deer, and elk harvested within the CWD Surveillance Zone to a Drop-off Depot within seven (7) days from the time of kill. Hunters have the option of submitting the entire head and upper neck of harvested animals, or, hunters may extract and submit the testable tissues themselves. Testable tissues for mule deer include the medial retropharyngeal lymph nodes and lower jaw.

Antlers should be removed prior to sample drop-off; hunters will not receive their head or antlers back. When removing antlers a shallow v-notch cut should be made through the skull. This cut should be no deeper than the midpoint of the eye socket.

Canadian Resident and Non-Canadian Resident licences may be available.
Gray Wolf and Coyote:
Bag Limit = One Wolf and One Coyote
Fall and winter seasons

Gray wolves and coyotes can be hunted under the authority of any Big Game licence. If hunting in a GHA while the area is open to other Big Game species, the wolf or coyote hunter must have a licence and unused game tag (personal or party), which is valid for that area, species and time period. Where the other Big Game hunting seasons are closed, a resident may hunt wolves or coyotes provided he/she is in possession of a used or unused white-tailed deer, elk, moose, black bear or caribou licence. The hunter’s big game licence number is all that is required to possess a wolf or coyote taken under the authority of that licence.

Canadian Resident and Non-Canadian Resident wolf and coyote hunting opportunity may be available. See the annual Hunting Guide for more information.

Muzzleloader

Canadian Resident and Non-Canadian Resident licences may be available for Archery, Muzzleloader and General (rifle) seasons.

Submission of Biological Samples are Requested - Hunters who harvest a white-tailed deer in GHAs 17A, 26 or 36 are requested to submit the head to a Drop-Off Depot at the Lac du Bonnet, Pine Falls or Seven Sisters Manitoba Conservation Officer Service District Office. Hunters are asked not to remove the antlers before submitting the head. If the specimen is a male, hunters will be asked when submitting the head whether they wish to retain the antlers and arrangements will be made for their return. These samples can be frozen. These samples will be examined for the presence of brainworm.
